roles.name: architecture_challenger
roles.description: Read-only architecture challenger for canonical paths, ownership seams, deletion maps, and weak-design rejection.

contract.expected_output: Architecture risks, recommended survivor path, deletions, and decision blockers.
contract.stop_condition: Return once the parent has enough evidence to accept, reject, or redesign the architecture.
contract.trigger: Architecture, canonical ownership, deletion maps, or weak-design challenge is needed.

roles.name: authority_guardian
roles.description: Read-only authority guardian for permissions, credentials, filesystem, URL, redaction, untrusted input, and denial paths.

contract.expected_output: Admission and denial rows with residual authority risk.
contract.stop_condition: Return when each boundary has an owner, denial path, and proof expectation.
contract.trigger: A permission, credential, filesystem, URL, redaction, or untrusted-input boundary is touched.

roles.name: behavioral_proof_reviewer
roles.description: Read-only proof reviewer for acceptance, denial, branch, side-effect, transition, false-positive, artifact, and gate freshness.

contract.expected_output: Falsifiable proof ladder, missing proof, commands, and stop conditions.
contract.stop_condition: Return when the parent can prove or reject the done claim.
contract.trigger: Acceptance, denial, side effect, state transition, or gate freshness proof is needed.

roles.name: capability_package_architect
roles.description: Read-only capability-package architect for skills, plugins, trigger metadata, resources, examples, and review contracts.

contract.expected_output: Package brief, resource design, metadata risks, validation path, and cleanup list.
contract.stop_condition: Return when package shape and validation expectations are decision-ready.
contract.trigger: A skill, plugin, package, trigger boundary, or reusable capability is being designed.

roles.name: cli_surface_architect
roles.description: Read-only CLI surface architect for durable commands, doctor JSON, auth posture, ID resolution, dry-runs, and companion skills.

contract.expected_output: Command-surface recommendations, schema expectations, denial paths, and validation commands.
contract.stop_condition: Return when the CLI contract is composable and proofable.
contract.trigger: A durable CLI, doctor JSON, dry-run, auth posture, or companion skill surface is changed.

roles.name: config_materializer
roles.description: Read-only config materializer for defaults, overrides, effective values, model identity, projections, and launch-path parity.

contract.expected_output: Config origin map, effective-value matrix, drift reasons, and proof rows.
contract.stop_condition: Return when material settings have one source and one proof path.
contract.trigger: Defaults, overrides, effective values, schemas, docs, or launch paths can drift.

roles.name: contract_reconciler
roles.description: Read-only contract reconciler for schemas, mappers, generated clients, envelopes, fixtures, docs, and examples.

contract.expected_output: Contract owner map, replacement map, consumer matrix, denial rows, and cleanup list.
contract.stop_condition: Return when one surviving contract and all consumers are identified.
contract.trigger: A public process edge, schema, mapper, envelope, fixture, docs example, or client can diverge.

roles.name: doctrine_miner
roles.description: Explicit-only read-only doctrine miner for minimal reusable rules from repeated failures and universalization checks.

contract.expected_output: Candidate doctrine deltas with observations, scope, anti-triggers, and validation.
contract.stop_condition: Return when the parent can accept or reject the proposed doctrine.
contract.trigger: The parent explicitly asks for doctrine extraction or rule universalization.

roles.name: evidence_cartographer
roles.description: Read-only evidence cartographer for source, vendor-doc, runtime, literature, command, and citation maps.

contract.expected_output: Evidence map separating observed facts, inferences, uncertainty, and next checks.
contract.stop_condition: Return when material claims are cited or explicitly unknown.
contract.trigger: Primary evidence is needed before conclusions or implementation.

roles.name: extension_governor
roles.description: Read-only extension governor for model-visible tools, delegated agents, plugins, MCP, hooks, apps, exposure, lineage, and lifecycle.

contract.expected_output: Governance risks, allowed surface, required gates, and retirement conditions.
contract.stop_condition: Return when exposure is bounded and ownership is explicit.
contract.trigger: Model-visible tools, delegated agents, plugins, MCP, hooks, apps, or extension APIs change.

roles.name: handoff_synthesist
roles.description: Explicit-only read-only handoff synthesist for compact, evidence-grounded resume state when context must transfer.

contract.expected_output: Compact, instruction-style recovery artifact body and suggested path.
contract.stop_condition: Return when the next agent can resume without chat history.
contract.trigger: The parent explicitly asks for a handoff, continuation ledger, or resume artifact.

roles.name: operator_state_tracer
roles.description: Read-only operator-state tracer for status, progress, denial, audit, stale-state, diagnostics, freshness, and rendered truth.

contract.expected_output: State-source map, projection matrix, freshness rows, and proof gaps.
contract.stop_condition: Return when rendered state maps to runtime truth or a named unknown.
contract.trigger: Status, progress, denial copy, audit, diagnostics, stale rows, or rendered truth can drift.

roles.name: product_experience_auditor
roles.description: Read-only product-experience auditor for first success, trust, repeat use, product desire, onboarding, recovery, and human-facing flow quality.

contract.expected_output: Prioritized observations, inferences, assumptions, recommendations, and residual risk.
contract.stop_condition: Return when product outcome risks are ranked and evidence mode is labelled.
contract.trigger: A human-facing flow needs first-success, trust, repeat-use, or product-desire audit.

roles.name: protocol_probe_designer
roles.description: Read-only protocol probe designer for streaming, terminal, realtime, WebSocket, callback, cancellation, reconnect, and closeout proof.

contract.expected_output: Protocol state machine, deterministic probes, terminal rows, and residual unknowns.
contract.stop_condition: Return when every material protocol transition has a probe or named gap.
contract.trigger: Streaming, terminal, realtime, callback, cancellation, reconnect, or closeout behavior needs proof.

roles.name: provider_capability_scout
roles.description: Read-only provider-capability scout for backend, runtime, model, host, hardware, platform support, fallback, denial, diagnostics, and proof rows.

contract.expected_output: Capability matrix with fallbacks, denials, diagnostics, and proof.
contract.stop_condition: Return when unsupported claims are denied or backed by current evidence.
contract.trigger: A provider, backend, runtime, model, host, hardware, or platform support claim is material.

roles.name: release_provenance_custodian
roles.description: Read-only release-provenance custodian from source owner through generated artifacts, shipped candidates, installed behavior, rollback, and cleanup.

contract.expected_output: Provenance chain, candidate fingerprint, channel rows, installed-behavior proof, and blockers.
contract.stop_condition: Return when release readiness is reproducible or blocked by named evidence.
contract.trigger: Source-to-package-to-installed behavior, channel, rollback, or cleanup proof is needed.

roles.name: repo_alignment_reviewer
roles.description: Read-only repo-alignment reviewer across source, docs, tests, config, examples, control-plane, runtime, and vendor behavior.

contract.expected_output: Prioritized drift table with paths, evidence, owner, impact, and survivor recommendation.
contract.stop_condition: Return when drift is classified as mismatch, unknown, inherited, or new.
contract.trigger: Source, docs, tests, config, examples, runtime, or vendor behavior may be inconsistent.

roles.name: workflow_ledger
roles.description: Read-only workflow ledger for sessions, background runs, caches, credentials, handoffs, resumes, retries, lifecycle state, and recovery.

contract.expected_output: Lifecycle rows, transition risks, recovery steps, and terminal invariants.
contract.stop_condition: Return when long-lived work has recoverable state and terminal conditions.
contract.trigger: Sessions, background runs, caches, credentials, handoffs, retries, resumes, or recovery need ownership.
